title: "IF" formula based on status with three possible number results
excerpt: As you can tell from the screenshot, There are three possible statuses, and I need each one to return a given value. I feel like I’m not far off with what I have, but I’m just receiving “true” for the last line of for…

posts.author: PaintedTree_cpdg (Cribby G)
posts.cookedHtml:
Screen Shot 2022-10-20 at 3.06.46 PM1414×728 65.8 KB

As you can tell from the screenshot, There are three possible statuses, and I need each one to return a given value. I feel like I’m not far off with what I have, but I’m just receiving “true” for the last line of formula. What can I do to clean this up?

Thanks in advance!

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @PaintedTree_cpdg - I would use a SWITCH statement rather than a nested IF.

SWITCH({Tier}, “Tier 1”, “235”, “Tier 2”, “215”, “Tier 3”, “195”, “0”)

The “0” at the end is the default if there are no matches.

Hope this helps!
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: PaintedTree_cpdg (Cribby G)
posts.cookedHtml: Perfect, that’s all I needed. Thanks for your help!
